calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion In Support Of Uber Technologies, Inc.S Petition To Quash Tax Collectors Subpoena For Driver Information (Discovery)
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Allison Davis, Esq. (415-276-6580) and Sanjay Nangia, Esq. (415-276-6577) of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP for Petitioner UBER TECHNOLOGIE INC.
Owen J. Clements, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3944) Chief of Special Litigation of City and County of San Francisco for Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties of it's questions. Petitioner and Respondent responds to the Court's inquiries. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

PETITIONER UBER TECHNOLOGIE INC.'S Petition To Quash Tax Collectors Subpoena For Driver Information is DENIED. Respondent City and County of San Francisco Office of the Treasurer-Tax Collector has shown that it is authorized by section 6.4-1 of the San Francisco Business and Tax Regulations Code to issue the administrative subpoena, the subpoena serves a valid purpose of investigating compliance with section 6.2-12 of the Code, and the information subpoenaed is pertinent to such a determination. Uber's quasi-constitutional challenges to the subpoena based on vagueness of the regulatory scheme and the prohibition of extraterritorial application of local ordinances are premature in light of Western Oil & Gas Assn. v. State Board of Equalization (1987) 44 Cal.3d 208, 214. Western Oil directs the court to only examine the Tax Collector's authority to issue the subpoena and not to determine the ultimate validity of the tax assessment. The subpoena is not overly broad and Uber has not shown that compliance would be unduly burdensome. Uber also failed to show that the driver information requested constitutes a protected trade secret or that disclosure would violate drivers' privacy or due process rights. Counsel to meet and confer regarding a compliance date and report back to the Court in writing on or before July 3, 2017. Order is signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/RBU)
